description: This figure presents a calculated pseudobinary phase diagram of the Fe–28Mn–10Al–0.8C–N system, with x-axis showing mass fraction of nitrogen ranging from 0.0 to 2.0 wt% and y-axis showing temperature ranging from ~700 K to 1800 K. Multiple phase regions are clearly labeled, covering equilibrium phase combinations of liquid phase (L), δ-ferrite, γ-austenite, α-ferrite, AlN, M₅C₂ and M₂₃C₆ precipitates. Calculated via Thermo-Calc software with the TCFE8 database, the diagram reveals that with increasing nitrogen content, the maximum formation temperature and stability temperature range of austenite significantly increase, while ferrite phase gradually disappears. As an austenite stabilizing element, nitrogen expands the austenite phase region and suppresses ferrite growth, which provides thermodynamic support for the experimentally observed reduction in ferrite content and austenite grain refinement in nitrogen-alloyed low-density steel.
